task_completion.description: Open the ASP.NET Core scaffolding dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add MVC Controller with views, using Entity Framework' dialog (or similar scaffolding window) is visible in Visual Studio.

task_completion.description: Select the correct Model and Data Context classes
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scaffolding dialog, the 'Model class' dropdown is set to 'WarehouseAsset (WarehouseDashboard.Models)' and the 'Data context class' dropdown is set to 'WarehouseContext (WarehouseDashboard.Data)'.
In the scaffolding dialog, the 'Controller name' text field is explicitly set to 'WarehouseAssetsController'.

task_completion.description: Verify the controller file was generated
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer shows 'WarehouseAssetsController.cs' has been created, typically under the 'Controllers' folder.

task_completion.description: Verify the CRUD views were generated
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer shows a new folder named 'WarehouseAssets' under the 'Views' folder, containing exactly five files: Create.cshtml, Delete.cshtml, Details.cshtml, Edit.cshtml, and Index.cshtml.
The Visual Studio 'Output' window at the bottom of the screen displays a successful build message (e.g., 'Build: 1 succeeded, 0 failed').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the scaffolding engine rather than manually creating files
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the scaffolding UI dialog or the Package Manager Console/terminal executing a scaffolding command, rather than the agent manually creating and typing out the files.

integrity.description: Verify the generated files contain actual boilerplate code
integrity.visual_evidence: If the generated controller or views are open in the editor, they should contain standard ASP.NET Core Entity Framework boilerplate code, not empty files or manually typed stubs.

integrity.description: Verify the build was genuinely executed in Visual Studio
integrity.visual_evidence: The build success message must appear in the genuine Visual Studio Output panel, showing standard MSBuild output logs, rather than a mocked text file or manually typed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected controller name is exactly 'WarehouseAssetsController.cs'. The scaffolding engine must generate exactly five views for the CRUD operations: 'Create.cshtml', 'Delete.cshtml', 'Details.cshtml', 'Edit.cshtml', and 'Index.cshtml'.
